title: Internet Control Message Protocol
description: Error reporting and diagnostic protocol used by ping and traceroute.
fullExplanation: ICMP is a supporting protocol for IP. It doesn't carry application data — instead, it reports errors and provides diagnostics. When a router can't forward a packet, it sends an ICMP 'Destination Unreachable' message back to the source. Ping uses ICMP Echo Request/Reply to test connectivity. Traceroute uses ICMP Time Exceeded messages to map the route. ICMP has no port number — it's part of IP itself (protocol number 1).

fields.name: Checksum

fields.name: Rest of Header
fields.description: Varies by message type (e.g., ID and Sequence for Echo)

messageTypes.name: Redirect
messageTypes.description: Router tells host to use different route

messageTypes.name: Echo Request
messageTypes.description: Ping - test if host is reachable

tools.name: ping
tools.description: Sends ICMP Echo Request, waits for Echo Reply

tools.name: traceroute / tracert
tools.description: Maps route by sending packets with increasing TTL

keyFacts: ICMP is part of IP layer, not a separate layer
No port numbers — not a transport protocol
Ping uses Echo Request (type 8) and Echo Reply (type 0)
Firewalls often block ICMP for security
ICMPv6 is used with IPv6, includes Neighbor Discovery
